The Chicago Routefinder — Direction 2
Editorial, typography-forward. Fleshed out from the original exploration and the Gemini refinements.
Cover · Contents
Design Direction StudyVol. IV · No. 112

The Chicago

Routefinder.

A typography-forward rethinking of the city transit app — one that reads like a printed almanac: cream paper stock, Fraunces serif display, rules and drop-caps, lamps instead of sirens, the lake and river drawn on the map for orientation. This document flows left-to-right: product → system → principles.

Contents
  1. The Product — nine screens of the PWA, mobile & desktop.
  2. The System — type, color, line pills, components, motion.
  3. The Principles — six notes on voice and restraint.
⟡ Printed in Chicago ⟡Direction II / of V — locked

06 · Alerts
Page 2 — Dispatches

Notices & Delays

Updated hourly from the dispatch feed.
REMajor
Sat · Sun

Weekend track work between Howard & Belmont

Northbound trains single-track through the weekend. Expect delays of ten to fifteen minutes; consider the Brown Line as an alternate.

REMinor
Ongoing

Jackson elevator out of service

Use Monroe or Roosevelt for step-free access. Service resumption expected by the 28th.

BLAdvisory
From May 1

New overnight schedule

Trains will run every fifteen minutes between 1 and 4 AM, seven days a week.

BRAdvisory
Tue

Addison reopens after platform repair

The inbound platform returns to service Tuesday morning. Thank you for your patience.

D · Principles
Specimen · D

Six Principles

I.
Read the city like a broadsheet
The interface is a daily paper, set fresh each morning. Every ride earns a column, every delay a dispatch.
II.
Lead with the numeral
Minutes are the hero. Typeset them large, in italic serif, with generous white space around.
III.
Italic softens, caps direct
Serif italic for voice, UI caps for wayfinding. Never the other way around.
IV.
Lamps, not sirens
Live state flickers at the edge. The interface should feel watched-over, not surveilled.
V.
Place before route
Lake on the right, river bending through. Stations labelled like landmarks.
VI.
A small red for consequence
Rust red is reserved for live state, delay, and the recommended mark — nothing decorative.
